prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of acrolein (8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', density-fitted PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2) and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). Geometry used as supplied (no re-optimization). SCF converged; integrity.trustworthy = true; both Fukui normalization checks passed (sum f+ = 1.0000, sum f− = 1.0000). No warnings reported. Headline result: the most electrophilic site is C4 (β-carbon, atom index 4, 1-based) with f+ = 0.64232102, and the most nucleophilic site is O1 (carbonyl oxygen) with f− = 0.39388616. The dual descriptor identifies C4 as the dominant electrophilic site (+0.5550), consistent with acrolein's known 1,4-Michael addition chemistry. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard acrolein.xyz. Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-171345_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
